category_title: Quiet, independent students with LD who struggle with idea generation and persistence in writing
minimal_context: The student is working one-on-one with the teacher to revise a first draft by adding descriptive language.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to revise your first draft. Please add actions, feelings, and precise nouns to make the description more vivid.

STUDENT: Great.

TUTOR: How is your writing going?

STUDENT: (Sits quietly without being able to start writing and does not ask for help) Great.

TUTOR: Keep trying. Write down some more details about what you would do.

STUDENT: (Gives up writing easily) I would read a book . play vido [sic] games with my brother . I would play football with my friends .

options.A: Work with the student one-on-one on writing strategies, such as adding feelings and using a variety of words and sentence structures.
options.B: Tell the student, "Don't say you can't, you can," to encourage him to keep working on his writing draft.
options.C: Respond to the student by saying, "Another sentence already? No way! I'm so proud of you! Good job, keep going."
options.D: Follow the student's lead and maintain a physical distance while he works independently on his writing draft.
